# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: Cross-account IAM role for CI/CD with wildcard trust across org accounts

Parameters:
  RoleName:
    Type: String
    Default: cicd-cross-account-role
  OrganizationId:
    Type: String
    Description: AWS Organizations ID (e.g., o-xxxxxxxxxx)
  TrustedPrincipalArnPattern:
    Type: String
    Default: "arn:aws:iam::*:role/*"
    Description: Principal ARN pattern allowed to assume this role (supports wildcards)

Resources:
  CiCdCrossAccountRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Ref RoleName
      Path: /
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: TrustOrgAccountsWithWildcardPrincipal
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: "*"
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
              - sts:TagSession
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref OrganizationId
              ArnLike:
                aws:PrincipalArn: !Ref TrustedPrincipalArnPattern
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: allow-sts-assume-role-only
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              - Sid: NoPermissionsByDefault
                Effect: Deny
                Action: "*"
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  Bool:
                    aws:ViaAWSService: "false"

Outputs:
  RoleArn:
    Value: !GetAtt CiCdCrossAccountRole.Arn
  RoleName:
    Value: !Ref CiCdCrossAccountRole
